evals.prompt: My landing page isn't converting — 400 visitors last week, 2 signups. It's at https://example-productivity-app.com. The headline is 'Meet FlowState — The Future of Productivity'. Can you review it?
evals.expected_output: An audit against the 7-section framework with pass/fail per section, specifically flagging the name-first headline as the top issue, 5 rewritten headline variants, and a prioritized top-3 fix list.
assertions: The audit evaluates the page against distinct framework sections (hero, problem, how-it-works, benefits, social proof, pricing, footer CTA)
The 'Meet [Product]' headline is flagged and outcome-first alternatives are provided
At least 5 headline variants are written with one recommended
The output ends with a prioritized short list of highest-impact fixes, not an unranked dump

evals.prompt: Write landing page copy for my new app: it's called Nestly, it helps landlords with 2-10 rental units track rent payments and maintenance requests without spreadsheets. Target user is small-time landlords who hate property management software because it's built for big companies. $19/mo, 14-day free trial.
evals.expected_output: Complete copy for all 7 sections (hero, problem, how it works, features→benefits, social proof plan, pricing, footer CTA) using the landing-page template, written in second person, with an asset checklist of needed visuals.
assertions: Copy is delivered for all 7 framework sections
The hero headline leads with the outcome for small landlords, not the product name
Feature bullets are written as benefits (what the landlord gets), not feature names
Pricing section includes the $19/mo and trial terms with an anchor comparison
The output notes what visual assets are needed per section
